rulings.Case Title: BRASIL SF, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. KEVIN O'CONNOR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Enforce Settlement And/Or Enter Judgment Under Ccp Sec 664.6 And Request For Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 7, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT KEVIN O'CONNOR Motion To Enforce Settlement And/Or Enter Judgment Under CCP Sec 664.6 And Request For Attorneys Fees. Denied without prejudice. If moving party files a new motion, it should provide sufficient evidence that Plaintiff is in breach of the settlement agreement. The moving papers did not provide evidence of tax liability. Alleged late payments need to be identified with specificity and supported with admissible evidence. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ROSA MARIA BUZON et al VS. ALEXANDER ROBERT VARTAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Deposition Of Pltf Req. For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 7, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT ALEXANDER VARTAN Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Deposition Of Pltf Req. For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: THE COURT DOES NOT HAVE A COPY OF THE MOVING PAPERS SO NO TENTATIVE WILL BE ISSUED. HOWEVER, NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED AND COUNSEL FOR THE MOVING PARTY SHOULD APPEAR AT THE HEARING ON THE 7TH WHERE THE COURT WILL EXAMINE THE MOVING PAPERS AND ISSUE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER WHICH SHOULD BE PREPARED BY COUNSEL FOR SUBMISSION TO THE COURT AT THAT TIME. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES ENGELBERG VS. ELLEN TSANG, AS TRUSTEE OF THE ARZA TRUST et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Defendant Charles Engelberg'S Motion For Leave To File A Motion To Compel
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 7, 2015, Line 2, Cross Defendant Charles Engelberg'S Motion For Leave To File A Motion To Compel Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: CROSS-DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A MOTION TO COMPEL IS GRANTED. CROSS-DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL AN ANSWER TO THE DEPOSITION QUESTION AND TO PRODUCE A COPY OF THE TRUST DOCUMENTS IS DENIED. CROSS-DEFENDANT IN HIS PLEADING DOES NOT DISPUTE THE FACT THE CROSS-COMPLAINANT IS THE TRUSTEE AND THERE IS INSUFFICIENT SHOWING THAT THE INFORMATION SOUGHT BY CROSS-DEFENDANT IS RELEVANT TO ANY OF THE ISSUES IN THE LITIGATION TRUMPTS THE BENEFICIARIES RIGHT TO PRIVACY. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED. COUNSEL FOR CROSS-COMPLAINANT SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT IT TO COUNSEL FOR CROSS-DEFENDANT FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER IT TO THE COURT FOR SIGNING AT THE HEARING. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: ELLEN TSANG VS. CHARLES ENGELBERG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Charles Engelberg'S Motion To Compel Responses To Defendant'S Request For Production Or Insepection Of Documents, Set One, And Monetary Sanction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 7, 2015, Line 3, Defendant Charles Engelberg'S Motion To Compel Responses To Defendant'S Request For Production Or Insepection Of Documents, Set One, And Monetary Sanction Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DEFENDANT CHARLES ENGELBERT'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OR INSPECTION OF DOCUMENTS IS DENIED AS TO REQUESTS NOS. 1-5 & 13-16 IT APPEARS FROM THE PAPERS THAT ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFF FILED OBJECTIONS TO ALL OF THESE REQUESTS SHE DID PRODUCE THE REQUESTED DOCUMENTS. AS TO REQUESTS NOS. 6-12 IT IS UNCLEAR AS TO WHETHER OR NOT SHE IS ASSERTING THAT NO SUCH VIDEOS EXIST. IF PLAINTIFF DOES HAVE ANY PHOTOGRAPHIC MATERIALS WHICH WOULD BE RESPONSIVE TO ANY OF THOSE REQUESTS, SHE IS ORDERED TO PRODUCE IT WITHIN TEN (10) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. NO MONATERY SANCITONS WILL BE IMPOSED. COUNSEL FOR THE MOVING PARTY SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT IT TO COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER IT TO THE COURT FOR SIGNING AT THE HEARING. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Joinder In Cross-Defendant Joseph William'S Demurrer And Demurrer To Tina Reynolds-Curcio'S First Amended Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 7, 2015, Line 11, DEFENDANT NURSEFINDERS, LLC, AMN HEALTHCARE INC. Joinder In Cross-Defendant Joseph William's Demurrer And Demurrer To Tina Reynolds-Curcio's First Amended Cross-Complaint. Overruled. The First Amended Cross-Complaint (FACC) sets forth the alleged harassment in sufficient detail. (FACC, par. 9.) The FACC adequately pleads the frequency, intensity, and/or timeliness of the alleged acts. (FACC, par. 9.) The FACC sufficiently pleads that Cross-Complainant exhausted her administrative remedies. (FACC, par. 8.) rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH WILLLIAMS VS. TINA REYNOLDS-CURCIO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 7, 2015, Line 11, DEFENDANT NURSEFINDERS, LLC, AMN HEALTHCARE INC. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained. (CCP sec. 430.10(e).) As to the first cause of action for unpaid wages, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. The First Amended Complaint ("FAC") explicitly pleads that the Incentive Plan provided bonuses to employees based upon performance. (FAC, par. 13.) The FAC also explicitly pleads that under the Incentive Plan, a bonus would be forfeited if an employee was put on a Performance Improvement Plan during the bonus measurement period. (FAC, par. 14.) The FAC pleads that Plaintiff was placed on a Performance Improvement Plan. (FAC, par. 25.) Thus, this cause of action fails as a matter of law, because pursuant to the terms of the Incentive Plan quoted in the FAC, Plaintiff was not entitled to a bonus for the quarter he was placed on a Performance Improvement Plan. (See Schacter v. Citigroup (2009) 47 Cal.4th 610, 621, "Eligibility to receive incentive compensation is properly determined by¿the plans' specific terms and general contract principles¿Only when an employee satisfies the condition(s) precedent to receiving incentive compensation¿can that employee be said to have earned the incentive compensation.") As to the second cause of action for breach of the implied covenant, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. The implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing does not create any obligations that are inconsistent with the express terms of a contract. (Nein v. HostPro. Inc. (2009) 174 Cal.App.4th 833, 852.) As to the third cause of action for wrongful termination, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. The FAC does not plead a violation of public policy, as an employer is permitted to set forth terms and conditions pertaining to the payment of a bonus, as discussed above. As to the fourth cause of action for defamation, the demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Plaintiff is given leave to amend to allege facts that support his respondeat superior theory raised in opposition to the demurrer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
